an open secret of "frontier" model training is that even without training on test, you can basically cheat by training on test lookalikes, enabling you to goalseek almost any benchmark number you want.

however when they are released open weights, 99% of the time the norm is that you do not get the datasets/rlenvs that would easily show you if someone was training on Temu Tbench, so there is plausible deniability. Alex and Omar discuss applying standard NLP distance metrics on hidden trajectories. There's no ultimate solution here, but they have some prelim explorations. {"builder": {"source": "x", "name": "Peter Yang", "handle": "petergyang", "bio": "Practical AI tutorials and interviews for busy people | Get my best AI skills and guides at https://t.co/6VAA6p81x6", "tweets": [{"id": "2079324894320603619", "text": "1) What https://t.co/kHWwdn0ZQs", "createdAt": "2026-07-20T21:57:41.000Z", "url": "https://x.com/petergyang/status/2079324894320603619", "likes": 52, "retweets": 1, "replies": 8, "isQuote": false, "quotedTweetId": null}, {"id": "2079273815004303477", "text": "Banning Chinese models will be the same self own as banning Chinese EVs.", "createdAt": "2026-07-20T18:34:43.000Z", "url": "https://x.com/petergyang/status/2079273815004303477", "likes": 1019, "retweets": 65, "replies": 71, "isQuote": false, "quotedTweetId": null}, {"id": "2079257646939742542", "text": "Use one agent to do the work and another to review it against a rubric.

@trq212 explains how this works for video shorts:

“For something like, ‘Is this a good video short or not?’ you don’t have a deterministic answer.

You should have a separate verification agent read a rubric, review the short, and give feedback.

We call it self-preferential bias. When a model prefers its own output, it’s going to be more lenient at verifying it.”

📌 Watch the full episode here: https://t.co/mMrt4D5qU7", "createdAt": "2026-07-20T17:30:28.000Z", "url": "https://x.com/petergyang/status/2079257646939742542", "likes": 47, "retweets": 5, "replies": 3, "isQuote": true, "quotedTweetId": "2078846124828545179"}]}, "tweets": [{"id": "2079257646939742542", "text": "Use one agent to do the work and another to review it against a rubric.

@trq212 explains how this works for video shorts:

“For something like, ‘Is this a good video short or not?’ you don’t have a deterministic answer.

You should have a separate verification agent read a rubric, review the short, and give feedback.

We call it self-preferential bias. When a model prefers its own output, it’s going to be more lenient at verifying it.”

📌 Watch the full episode here: https://t.co/mMrt4D5qU7", "createdAt": "2026-07-20T17:30:28.000Z", "url": "https://x.com/petergyang/status/2079257646939742542", "likes": 47, "retweets": 5, "replies": 3, "isQuote": true, "quotedTweetId": "2078846124828545179"}]}

title: Aaron Levie: Multi-model agentic systems clearly are the future. Great post by cursor. Their new research shows that a fro...
summary: Follow Builders 记录到 Aaron Levie（@levie） 在 X 上的最新动态。简介：ceo @box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI。要点：1. Multi-model agentic systems clearly are the future. Great post by cursor. Their new research shows that a frontier model as a planner and orchestrator with a workhorse cheaper mod...
raw_text:
{"builder": {"source": "x", "name": "Aaron Levie", "handle": "levie", "bio": "ceo @box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI", "tweets": [{"id": "2079402164988895293", "text": "Multi-model agentic systems clearly are the future. Great post by cursor. Their new research shows that a frontier model as a planner and orchestrator with a workhorse cheaper model can materially lower the cost of total tokens on a project, resulting in a 15X cost improvement.

“Few moments in a large task genuinely require frontier intelligence, such as the original decomposition, the design decisions, and certain trade-offs. Once a frontier planner has collapsed the ambiguity into a detailed, explicit instruction, less expensive models simply have to follow it.”

This is increasingly becoming the core design pattern of complex agents, because the tokens used on large amounts of work on many tasks don’t require the same intelligence threshold as the planning step. By routing to different models based on where you are in the task, you get much greater efficiency overall. 

This provides the template for where the applied layer in AI will differentiate. You can only drive this efficiency if you know the domain well and have the ability to work with multiple model tiers; companies that can do this will across coding, finance, legal, healthcare, life sciences, and other critical spaces will be in a strong position to get larger workloads that would otherwise be too expensive for the customer to deploy.", "createdAt": "2026-07-21T03:04:44.000Z", "url": "https://x.com/levie/status/2079402164988895293", "likes": 206, "retweets": 29, "replies": 37, "isQuote": true, "quotedTweetId": "2079256614238814551"}]}, "tweets": [{"id": "2079402164988895293", "text": "Multi-model agentic systems clearly are the future. Great post by cursor. Their new research shows that a frontier model as a planner and orchestrator with a workhorse cheaper model can materially lower the cost of total tokens on a project, resulting in a 15X cost improvement.

“Few moments in a large task genuinely require frontier intelligence, such as the original decomposition, the design decisions, and certain trade-offs. Once a frontier planner has collapsed the ambiguity into a detailed, explicit instruction, less expensive models simply have to follow it.”

This is increasingly becoming the core design pattern of complex agents, because the tokens used on large amounts of work on many tasks don’t require the same intelligence threshold as the planning step. By routing to different models based on where you are in the task, you get much greater efficiency overall. 

This provides the template for where the applied layer in AI will differentiate. You can only drive this efficiency if you know the domain well and have the ability to work with multiple model tiers; companies that can do this will across coding, finance, legal, healthcare, life sciences, and other critical spaces will be in a st...

raw_text:
{"builder": {"source": "x", "name": "Nikunj Kothari", "handle": "nikunj", "bio": "partner @fpvventures - investing in seed/A. previous: early hire @meter, @opendoor, @atlassian & others. love @shimoleejhaveri + 👦👧", "tweets": [{"id": "2079328912912355470", "text": "Many founders in the last 18 months are going to realize that just because there are no “moats” in AI left anymore doesn’t mean “scale and capital” become your main moat.

We have seen this movie before.. all of us will benefit from reading a little bit of history.

Webvan
Groupon
MySpace
Yahoo
AltaVista
Blockbuster
Nokia
(zombiecorns of 2021)

Each one was structurally and capital-wise in a very good position, but eventually got beat by a company with a much better unique insight. Or just collapsed under the weight of their own scale. 

This is the needle founders have to thread: find a unique insight worth a 10+ year journey, while being prudent enough to not let capital & scale become a substitute for it.", "createdAt": "2026-07-20T22:13:39.000Z", "url": "https://x.com/nikunj/status/2079328912912355470", "likes": 107, "retweets": 5, "replies": 13, "isQuote": false, "quotedTweetId": null}, {"id": "2079211477127291350", "text": "This VC to “fast growing startup” train is insane to watch.. another three people just this past week. 

I guess it’s similar to the IB/consultant to BizOps train I saw in 2012. 

Top title for this cohort? 

Special projects ⚡️ https://t.co/FqarRFC8Iw", "createdAt": "2026-07-20T14:27:00.000Z", "url": "https://x.com/nikunj/status/2079211477127291350", "likes": 79, "retweets": 0, "replies": 6, "isQuote": true, "quotedTweetId": "2066701833964531736"}]}, "tweets": [{"id": "2079328912912355470", "text": "Many founders in the last 18 months are going to realize that just because there are no “moats” in AI left anymore doesn’t mean “scale and capital” become your main moat.

We have seen this movie before.. all of us will benefit from reading a little bit of history.

Webvan
Groupon
MySpace
Yahoo
AltaVista
Blockbuster
Nokia
(zombiecorns of 2021)

Each one was structurally and capital-wise in a very good position, but eventually got beat by a company with a much better unique insight. Or just collapsed under the weight of their own scale. 

This is the needle founders have to thread: find a unique insight worth a 10+ year journey, while being prudent enough to not let capital & scale become a substitute for it.", "createdAt": "2026-07-20T22:13:39.000Z", "url": "https://x.com/nikunj/status/2079328912912355470", "likes": 107, "retweets": 5, "replies": 13, "isQuote": false, "quotedTweetId": null}]}
